content.title: Getting Started
content.description:
A quick orientation for new users. Everything you need to start using Hobson — the rest you can discover from within the system itself.

---

## Everything Is an Item

In Hobson, there are no files, folders, or apps — just **items**. Notes, tasks, code, views, types, even this documentation — all items. Every item has a type, a set of content fields, and can link to other items. This uniformity means you only need to learn one set of interactions.

## The Spatial Canvas

When you open Hobson, you're looking at the [spatial canvas](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9) — a freeform workspace where items appear as draggable, resizable windows. You can pan the canvas by dragging the background and zoom with the scroll wheel. Think of it as an infinite desk where you arrange your stuff.

## Right-Click Everything

The **context menu** is your primary tool. Right-click on:

- **An item** — to see actions: edit, delete, change view, inspect, link, and more
- **The background** — to create new items, paste, or open search
- **A selection** of items — to act on multiple items at once

When in doubt, right-click. The menu is context-sensitive and will show you what's possible. See [Context Menus](item://c0c0c0c0-0034-0000-0000-000000000000) for details.

## Creating Items

Right-click the canvas background and choose **New Item**. Pick a type (note, task, or any custom type) and a new item appears on the canvas. Click into it to start editing.

## Navigating Into Items

Items can contain other items via [attachments](item://c0c0c0c0-0005-0000-0000-000000000000). Double-click an item (or use its context menu) to **navigate into** it, making it the root of your canvas. The breadcrumb bar at the top shows your path — click any ancestor to go back up. See [Navigation](item://c0c0c0c0-0003-0000-0000-000000000000).

## Links and Transclusion

Items can reference each other in two ways:

- **Links** — In any markdown field, write `[link text](item://guid)` to create a clickable link to another item.
- **Transclusion** — Write `![](item://guid)` to **embed** another item inline, rendered live with its own view.

Transclusion can also target specific fields or line ranges. See [Transclusion](item://c0c0c0c0-0035-0000-0000-000000000000) for the full syntax.

## Tags and Related Items

Any item can act as a [tag](item://c0c0c0c0-0021-0000-0000-000000000000). Tagging is lightweight classification — add tags via an item's context menu or its edit view. Tags help you find and filter items without imposing a rigid hierarchy. An item's context menu also shows **related items** (items that share tags or link to it).

## Search

Press **Cmd+K** (or Ctrl+K) to open the **item palette** — a fast search over all items by name. Start typing to filter, then press Enter to navigate to the result or add it to your canvas.

## The REPL

Press **Esc** to toggle the REPL — an interactive console with full access to the kernel API. You can query items, create them programmatically, test code, or inspect system state. It's entirely optional for day-to-day use, but powerful when you need it. See [kernel:repl](item://33333333-6666-0000-0000-000000000000).

## Safe Mode

If something goes wrong (a bad code item, a broken view), add `?safe=1` to the URL. This boots only the kernel — no user code runs — so you can fix or delete the offending item. See [URL Parameters](item://c0c0c0c0-0031-0000-0000-000000000000).

## Where to Go From Here

- [Core Concepts](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000002) — Items, types, code, libraries, views
- [Architecture Overview](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000003) — How the system is built
- [Philosophy & Inspirations](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000001) — Why Hobson works the way it does
- [Views & Rendering](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000004) — How items get displayed, and how to build custom views
